{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ EXECUTIVE SUMMARY · the one-page version ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

Executive summary

For the busy reviewer

Clause 44 of Form 3CD requires every assessee under tax-audit to break up its total expenditure on goods and services for the year into four GST-status cohorts. The module ingests a Tally JSON export of the books, classifies each ledger automatically using its parent group and prior-year mapping, lets the auditor override the few exceptions, and produces a single working-paper that maps 1-for-1 onto the Clause 44 table.

  • Input  Tally JSON export (Display > Statements of Accounts > Ledger Vouchers, exported as JSON).
  • Effort  ~10 minutes per division for a clean books-set; ~25 minutes when manual classification is required.
  • Output  Excel working-paper with five sheets: Summary, Exempt, Non-GST, Composition, Registered — each with ledger-level proofs that tie to the books.
  • Audit assertion supported  Completeness & classification of expenditure for Clause 44 reporting.

The remainder of this document is the click-by-click walkthrough for the person doing the work — typically the articled clerk preparing the schedule for partner review.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ TOC ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

What's inside

  1. Regulatory primer — what Clause 44 actually asks
  2. The four cohorts, demystified
  3. Before you start — prerequisites
  4. Step-by-step walkthrough
  5. Reading the output workbook
  6. Edge cases & common pitfalls
  7. Frequently asked questions
  8. Glossary
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 1 · REGULATORY PRIMER ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

1 · Regulatory primer

Clause 44 of Form 3CD was inserted by the Income-Tax (8th Amendment) Rules, 2018 to capture the GST-classification breakup of every rupee debited to the Profit & Loss account (and capital expenditure routed through purchase ledgers). The reporting requirement was kept in abeyance until FY 2021-22 and is now mandatory for all tax-audit cases.

The clause requires a seven-column table where each row is one natural head of expense and the columns split that head's total amount across:

  1. Total expenditure A
  2. Expenditure on entities not registered under GST — further split into:
    • Relating to goods/services exempt from GST B1
    • Relating to entities falling under the Composition scheme B2
    • Other registered entities B3
    • Total payments to non-registered entities B4
  3. Total payment to registered entities (= A − B4)
Auditor's note The four cohorts the module produces (Exempt · Non-GST · Composition · Registered) collapse cleanly onto the seven-column table. The Excel Summary sheet is laid out exactly the way the 3CD utility expects.
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 2 · THE FOUR COHORTS ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

2 · The four cohorts, demystified

The module classifies every ledger into one — and only one — of these:

Exempt

Goods/services exempt under GST

Salaries, statutory dues, interest, dividend, donations, insurance premiums for life and health (specified categories), bank charges, etc. Anything covered by the GST exemption notifications.

Non-GST

Outside the GST net altogether

Petrol & diesel, electricity, alcoholic liquor for human consumption, stamp duty & registration, court fees, fines & penalties, foreign-supplier transactions where IGST is paid under reverse charge separately.

Composition

Vendor under composition scheme

Smaller suppliers (turnover ≤ ₹1.5 Cr; ≤ ₹50 L for services) who pay GST at concessional flat rates and cannot pass on input credit. Their invoices show "Composition taxable person, not eligible to collect tax on supplies".

Registered

Vendor with regular GSTIN

Default cohort. Any supplier holding a regular GST registration and charging tax in the normal manner. The module funnels everything that isn't Exempt / Non-GST / Composition into this bucket.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 3 · PRE-REQUISITES ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

3 · Before you start

3.1 The Tally export you'll need

Open the client's books in Tally Prime (or ERP 9) and export the ledger-vouchers JSON for the audit period.

StepTally menu path
1Gateway of Tally → Display More Reports → Account Books → Ledger
2Set the period using Alt + F2 to the full audit year (e.g. 1-Apr-2024 to 31-Mar-2025)
3Press Ctrl + E (Export). Format: JSON. Show profit / Show inventory: No. Range: All ledgers.
4Save the file. The default name is {COMPANY}_DAYBOOK_{from}-{to}.json — keep it as-is; the module reads metadata from the filename.
Pro tip The same JSON powers Clause 44 and 43B(h) and the Balance Confirmation module. Export it once, reuse it three times.

3.2 Client master & period

  • The client must already exist in All Clients. If not, click + Add Client on the dashboard first — that takes 30 seconds.
  • For multi-division clients, ensure each division has been added (Settings → Divisions). Tally usually exports one JSON per division.
  • The audit period should match the year your Tally export covers. Default is the previous FY.

3.3 Access & permissions

Any user with the Auditor role can run the module. Admin rights are not required. The output never leaves your tenant database.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 4 · WALKTHROUGH ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

4 · Step-by-step walkthrough

1

Pick the client & create a run

From All Clients, open the client (e.g. ABC Textile Mills) → Utilities → click the Clause 44 Schedule card.

You land on the Choose Period screen. Select the FY (defaults to the prior year), choose a division if the client is multi-division, and click Start a new run.

What happens under the hood A run is just a workspace tied to (client × period × division). You can keep multiple runs against the same period — useful when you want to A/B compare classifications or revisit after audit-team feedback.
2

Upload the Tally JSON

The upload step accepts a single .json file. Drag-drop or click to browse. Files up to ~100 MB are supported (typical exports run 5-25 MB).

The system reads the file in the browser, ships a parsed payload to the server, and persists it against the run. You'll see a parse confirmation toast and the row count at the bottom of the panel.

If the upload fails 99% of upload failures are caused by the JSON being exported with Show profit = Yes. Re-export with profit/inventory toggles set to No, and try again. The other 1% is browsers blocking large clipboard pastes — drag-drop the file instead.
3

Review the auto-mapping

After upload you land on the Mapping screen. Two side-by-side columns:

ColumnWhat it showsDefault state
Suggested expense ledgersEvery ledger the engine considers a P&L expense based on parent-group (Indirect Expenses, Direct Expenses, Purchase Accounts, etc.).All checked
Excluded ledgersAsset / liability / income ledgers that won't appear in the schedule.All un-checked

You can:

  • Tick a ledger in the right column to include it (e.g. a "Repairs - Capital" ledger that the previous CA had treated as expense).
  • Un-tick a ledger in the left column to exclude it (e.g. an inter-branch transfer).
  • Use the search bar to jump to a specific ledger by name or parent group.
  • Click Select Suggested / Clear to reset bulk state.
Pro tip — ride the prior-year mapping If the same client was processed last year, the module remembers your overrides and applies them automatically. You'll see a small "recalled from FY 2023-24" badge on each ledger that picked up a prior-year decision.
4

Generate the schedule

Click Generate Run at the bottom of the mapping screen. The engine:

  1. Aggregates voucher-level debits/credits per ledger for the chosen period.
  2. Routes each ledger to one of the four cohorts using a 200+ rule rule-set (parent-group keywords, vendor-name patterns, GST treatment hints, prior-year mapping).
  3. Computes the four totals + the "Total non-registered" subtotal.
  4. Persists the result and routes you to the Report screen.

For a typical clean books-set with 800-1500 ledgers this takes 4-7 seconds.

5

Resolve exceptions

The Report screen highlights any ledger the classifier couldn't decide with high confidence — typically 5-30 rows out of a 1000-ledger book. They appear in the Exceptions drawer at the top. Each row shows:

  • Ledger name & parent group
  • The classifier's tentative cohort
  • The reasoning trace (which rules fired, prior-year decision if any)
  • A four-radio "override" picker — Exempt / Non-GST / Composition / Registered

Resolve every exception. The summary recomputes live as you click. Once the drawer is empty, the schedule is reviewable.

Pitfall — bank charges Bank charges and processing fees are Exempt (Notification 12/2017-CT(R) entry 27). The classifier sometimes nudges them to Registered when the bank ledger is named like a vendor (e.g. "HDFC Bank Charges A/c"). Override these to Exempt — the workpaper auto-recomputes the cohort totals.
6

Download the working paper

Top-right of the Report screen, click Export Excel. You get a single .xlsx with five sheets — described in detail in the next section.

If you also want a single-page PDF for the audit file, use the Print Schedule (PDF) action on the same toolbar. The PDF carries the firm header and is signed-off-ready.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 5 · OUTPUT ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

5 · Reading the output workbook

SheetContentsTie to 3CD
SummaryThe 4-cohort total + B4 + Registered total. Lays out exactly the way the 3CD utility expects to receive it.Direct copy-paste into the Clause 44 table.
ExemptEvery ledger in the Exempt cohort with parent group, debit/credit, net, and the override flag if any.Backing for column B1.
Non-GSTPetrol/diesel/electricity/stamp duty etc. with the same shape.Backing for the "outside GST" disclosure.
CompositionVendor-level breakup — usually small.Backing for column B2.
RegisteredThe largest sheet. Every regular-GST vendor ledger.Backing for "Total registered" = A − B4.

What the Summary sheet looks like

Five columns, one row per natural head. The column headers exactly match 3CD column letters:

Head of expenseTotal (A)Exempt (B1)Composition (B2)Other registered (B3)Non-registered (B4)Registered (A − B4)
Purchase of raw material4,12,68,000—2,18,0004,08,12,0002,38,0004,10,30,000
Power & fuel1,82,40,000———1,82,40,000—
Salaries & wages2,11,06,5002,11,06,500——2,11,06,500—
… and so on …
{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 6 · EDGE CASES ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

6 · Edge cases & common pitfalls

6.1 Capital expenditure routed through revenue ledgers

Sometimes a fixed-asset purchase finds its way into a "Repairs & Maintenance" ledger. Clause 44 only asks about expenditure debited to P&L — capital additions should ride the Fixed Assets schedule, not Clause 44. Use the Exclude ticking on the Mapping screen to remove these entries.

6.2 Reimbursements & recoveries

Reimbursement of travel, telephone, etc. recovered from group companies is not "expenditure on goods/services". Exclude such ledgers via the override. The work-paper carries a Notes column where you should record the basis ("Reimbursed by ABC Holdings").

6.3 RCM payments

GST paid under reverse-charge by the assessee on services from unregistered/foreign vendors is itself Non-GST from the vendor's standpoint — the vendor's ledger goes to Non-Registered (B3) and the GST-RCM ledger goes to Exempt. The classifier handles this automatically when it finds an RCM tag.

6.4 Foreign-currency vendors

Imports paid via LC: cohort = Non-Registered. The bank charges & LC commission ledgers go separately to Exempt as bank-related services.

6.5 Salaries vs Director Remuneration

Both go to Exempt — services rendered by an employee/director to their employer are out of GST scope (Schedule III). The classifier keys on the parent group "Salaries", "Director Remuneration", "Wages".

6.6 Donations under CSR

If donated in kind via purchase, the original purchase ledger's cohort applies. If a cash donation, cohort = Exempt.

6.7 What about deferred items routed via prepaid?

Annual maintenance contracts, insurance premia, etc. that you know are routed via "Prepaid Expenses" — only the period-amortised portion is in the P&L. The classifier picks up only the P&L hit. The prepaid balance sheet movement is out of scope.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 7 · FAQ ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

7 · Frequently asked questions

How accurate is the auto-classification?

Across our test pool of 30+ tax-audit clients (mid-cap manufacturing, services, trading), the classifier hits 92-97% accuracy on first pass. The remaining 3-8% surface as Exceptions — which is by design. The auditor — not the engine — bears Clause 44 responsibility.

Can I reuse a run from last year?

Yes. The mapping screen carries a "Recall FY 2023-24 mapping" toggle. When on, every prior-year override re-applies before you even see the screen.

What if Tally has been migrated mid-year?

Export each segment (Apr-Aug from old company, Sep-Mar from new) as separate JSONs and create two runs in the same period. Combine the Summary sheets manually — they're additive.

Does the module support Zoho/Busy/SAP?

Today only Tally JSON is supported natively. For Zoho Books / Busy, export the trial-balance and ledger-by-ledger CSV; we have a CSV adapter in beta — write to {{ support_email }} if you'd like access.

Does it leak data?

No. The Tally JSON is parsed and stored against your tenant DB. The classifier rules are local. No call goes to any external LLM unless you explicitly use the OCR-attach feature in Fixed Assets — which is different and uses Gemini Vision under your Emergent LLM key.

What happens when I delete a run?

The run, its ledger mappings, and the Excel cache are all removed. The Tally JSON is also deleted. Audit-trail entries (who deleted, when) survive in the admin log.

Can two articled clerks work on the same run simultaneously?

Yes — the Mapping and Report screens are real-time and collaborative. The exceptions drawer locks each row to whoever is editing it for 30 seconds.

Where do I report a wrong classification?

Use the Suggest correction button on the Mapping screen. Your override + reasoning ships to the AssureAI rules team and improves the next release for everyone.

{# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 8 · GLOSSARY ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ #}

8 · Glossary

3CD
Annexure to Form 3CA / 3CB — the tax-audit report under section 44AB.
Clause 44
The clause inside 3CD that captures the GST-status breakup of total expenditure.
Cohort
Our internal name for one of the four mutually-exclusive classification buckets.
Composition scheme
A simplified GST regime for small suppliers (turnover ≤ ₹1.5 Cr; ≤ ₹50 L for services). Composition vendors charge concessional flat rates and cannot pass on input credit.
Exempt supply
Supply that is exempt from GST — explicitly notified as nil-rated, exempted, or non-taxable.
Non-GST
Goods/services kept outside the GST net altogether (petrol, diesel, electricity, alcohol for human consumption, stamp duty, court fees).
Parent group
The Tally classification ladder above an individual ledger — e.g. Indirect Expenses > Selling & Distribution.
Run
A workspace tied to (client × period × division). Holds the upload, mapping decisions and the generated workpaper.
RCM
Reverse-charge mechanism — GST liability sits on the recipient instead of the supplier, typically for legal/manpower services and imports.
{% endblock %}
